title: Fix Connect Bot Wizard Ed25519 keypair generation

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` so `handleGenerateKeypair` no longer uses Web Crypto Ed25519 and instead generates the Ed25519 keypair using `@noble/ed25519` exactly as follows: `import * as ed from '@noble/ed25519'; const privateKey = ed.utils.randomPrivateKey(); const publicKey = await ed.getPublicKeyAsync(privateKey);`. Display the private key as a hex string (BOT_PRIVATE_KEY) and keep the public key as a `Uint8Array` for sending to the backend. If `@noble/ed25519` is not available at runtime/build-time, fall back to generating a 32-byte random seed and produce a placeholder derived public key, including the exact comment: `// TODO: Replace with real Ed25519 derivation in production.`. Do not change any other wizard UI steps, blur/copy behavior, warning text, or the existing `linkMutation` call flow.

quotes: MAIN BLOCKER Only one failure: Connect Bot Wizard keypair generation must switch to @noble/ed25519 exactly as defined in REQ‑24.

acceptanceCriteria: In `ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx`, `handleGenerateKeypair` does not call `window.crypto.subtle.generateKey` (or any Web Crypto Ed25519 generation) on the normal path.

On the normal path, `privateKey` is generated with `ed.utils.randomPrivateKey()` and `publicKey` is derived using `await ed.getPublicKeyAsync(privateKey)` (public key is not independent random bytes).

The private key displayed and copied is a hex string derived from the generated private key (BOT_PRIVATE_KEY), and the public key remains a `Uint8Array` used by the existing `linkMutation.mutateAsync(keypair.publicKey)` flow.

If noble is unavailable, the code falls back to a 32-byte random seed and produces a placeholder derived public key, and the source includes the exact comment `// TODO: Replace with real Ed25519 derivation in production.`.

The modal’s step flow remains unchanged (generate → reveal → link → url → done), and the warning text remains exactly: `Paste this into your Python Bot's .env file as BOT_PRIVATE_KEY. This will not be shown again.`
The private key input remains blurred by default and copyable, and sensitive data is still cleared on close as currently implemented.
No new console errors are introduced when generating keys, linking the public key, or saving the bot URL.

constraints: Frontend immutable paths must not be edited: `frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`, `frontend/src/components/ui/*`.
Keep all user-facing text in English.
Do not change any other wizard behavior beyond the key generation implementation (steps, blur/copy behavior, existing mutation flows).
nonGoals: Do not change backend signature verification or uplink logic.
Do not refactor routing, settings tabs, or subscription/Stripe UI as part of this fix.
Do not modify `backend/stripe/stripe.mo` or any backend code for this request.
